reason: Response B exhibits stylistic hallmarks more consistent with Claude Haiku: shorter, punchier sentences, a more conversational and direct tone, and a memorable punchy closing ("It is the most useful number built from nothing. Zero takes up no space and holds the whole system together."). Response A reads more like a longer-form, structured essay with a measured academic tone that feels more characteristic of a larger model like Sonnet or Opus. Haiku tends toward concise, energetic prose with deliberate rhetorical rhythm, which B demonstrates more clearly.
